title: Content, padding, border and margin

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Content, padding, border and margin; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Content, padding, border and margin
sources.claim: Each box has content, padding, border, and margin; box-sizing controls whether declared dimensions include padding and border.

sources.claim: Each box has content, padding, border, and margin; box-sizing controls whether declared dimensions include padding and border.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Each box has content, padding, border, and margin; box-sizing controls whether declared dimensions include padding and border.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: With border-box, a 200px width includes horizontal padding and border instead of producing a wider outer border box. Your goal is to trace and repair unexpected element size using the browser box-model inspector. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Rules, declarations and values. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Each box has content, padding, border, and margin; box-sizing controls whether declared dimensions include padding and border. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Margins can collapse in block flow, inline boxes differ, and intrinsic sizing or min-size constraints can override naive arithmetic. This draft therefore rejects: Declared width always equals the final outer width of an element.

includes: Calculate used box dimensions and apply border-box sizing deliberately.
Trace and repair unexpected element size using the browser box-model inspector.
excludes: Margins can collapse in block flow, inline boxes differ, and intrinsic sizing or min-size constraints can override naive arithmetic.

outcomes: Trace and repair unexpected element size using the browser box-model inspector.

examples.title: Content, padding, border and margin: concrete case
examples.situation: With border-box, a 200px width includes horizontal padding and border instead of producing a wider outer border box.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: With border-box, a 200px width includes horizontal padding and border instead of producing a wider outer border box.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Inspect box-sizing and every box edge; width does not necessarily equal the final outer size.
examples.result: The result follows because Inspect box-sizing and every box edge; width does not necessarily equal the final outer size.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Content, padding, border and margin, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Content, padding, border and margin before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
